page: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-48 is a general-purpose virtual machine configured with 48 vCPUs and 192 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz, this server features 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, resulting in 2.0 threads per core. The x86_64 architecture supports Advanced Vector Extensions, including AVX-512, and Cryptography instructions like AES. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage and CPU-based execution.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong memory performance, with uncached large-block read-only memory bandwidth reaching 186.37 GB/sec and a strong Passmark memory score, both placing in the top 25%. However, computational benchmarks like stress-ng single-core and multi-core fall into the average tier. Large language model inference speeds for text generation are weak, scoring in the bottom 25% for both 135M and 7B models, though prompt processing speeds remain average.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the n2-standard-48 offers a balanced resource density of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, making it a stable option for workloads requiring consistent memory access without the premium cost of memory-optimized tiers. The primary tradeoffs include the lack of local NVMe storage and weak performance in generative AI tasks. This server is best suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-48 is a general-purpose server featuring 48 vCPUs and 192 GB of RAM, powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture with 24 physical cores and hyper-threading, it supports AVX-512 and AES instructions. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong memory performance, with uncached large-block read-only memory bandwidth of 186.37 GB/sec. However, CPU-bound benchmarks and LLM text generation speeds are average to weak. Qualitatively, the server provides balanced resource density for workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. It is suited for static web serving, database operations, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-48 is a general-purpose server with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU. It features strong memory bandwidth but lacks local storage and GPUs.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-48 features 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and strong memory bandwidth for general-purpose enterprise workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring forty-eight dedicated vCPUs, strong memory bandwidth, and Intel Xeon architecture.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 192 GB RAM running on a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor
Strong uncached memory read bandwidth of 186.37 GB/sec
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Supports AVX-512 and AES hardware-accelerated instructions
Suited for static web serving, database operations, and software compilation
